page: Amazon Web Services c5d.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-speed instance store volumes directly attached to the host.

Performance benchmarks place the c5d.4xlarge consistently in the average tier for most operations. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1405.49 and a multi-core score of 14734.77. In memory performance, it registers a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 823.29 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 38.76 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the server demonstrates solid capabilities with a Geekbench software compilation score of 12847.0 and a ray tracing score of 12276.0. However, single-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25% at 8.73 GB/sec, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression recovers to an average tier of 35.8 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5d.4xlarge offers a balanced profile for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed local scratch space without the overhead of network-attached storage. The 400 GB local NVMe SSD eliminates external storage latency, making it highly suitable for applications requiring rapid read and write cycles. With a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps and average database operations performance of 5858.93 on Passmark, this instance is well-suited for batch processing, distributed analytics, ad-hoc compilation, and high-throughput web serving. It also supports light machine learning tasks, delivering 141.67 tokens/sec for text generation on a small 135M LLM, though performance drops to a weak 22.77 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server features 400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a 5 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark results place the instance in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14734.77 and a Passmark database operations score of 5858.93. While single-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck at 8.73 GB/sec, the server excels in multi-threaded tasks and software compilation. The combination of dedicated compute resources and high-speed local storage makes the c5d.4xlarge highly efficient for batch processing, software compilation, web serving, and database workloads requiring low-latency scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and 400 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced performance for database and batch workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.4xlarge features 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding database and batch processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Average-tier performance in software compilation and database operations
Best suited for batch processing, web serving, and low-latency database tasks
